source.submodule: Returning objects - Static, nested and inner classes

topicTags: Returning objects - Static, nested and inner classes

content.problemStatement:
In Java, a nested class is a class that is defined within another class. Nested classes are also sometimes referred to as inner classes. They are used to logically group classes within the scope of another class and can have access to the private members of the outer class. There are several types of nested classes in Java, including static nested classes, non-static (inner) classes, and local classes. In this example, you will see how to create a static nested class and an inner class:

In the example above, we have an `OuterClass` that contains both a static nested class (`StaticNestedClass`) and an inner class (`InnerClass`).

1. `StaticNestedClass`: This class is declared as `static`, and you can create instances of it without creating an instance of the outer class. It can only access static members of the outer class.

2. `InnerClass`: This class is an inner class or non-static nested class. It requires an instance of the outer class to be created. It can access both static and non-static members of the outer class.

In the `main` method, you can see how to create instances of both the static nested class and the inner class. The inner class can access the `outerField` of the outer class, which is a private member.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
